registry.description: Weakness is the condition of being without sufficient strength to accomplish what is needed — the experience of one's own limitation and insufficiency. The Greek vocabulary here (astheneia) covers physical, moral, and spiritual weakness. In the New Testament, weakness is reframed rather than merely lamented: Paul's discovery is that God's power is most clearly visible in human weakness, that the strength that matters is not one's own. Weakness in this frame is not a problem to be solved but a condition to be inhabited honestly, which creates the space for divine strength.

terms.medium_def:
weakness, illness, infirmity 
want of strength, weakness, feebleness 1Cor. 15:43; bodily infirmity, illness, state of ill health, sickness, Mt. 8:17; Lk. 5:15; metaphorically infirmity, frailty, imperfection, intellectual and moral, Rom. 6:19; 1Cor. 2:3; Heb. 5:2; 7:28; suffering, affliction, distress, calamity, Rom. 8:26
terms.lsj_entry:
ἀσθέν-εια, ἡ, 
want of strength, weakness, [Refs 5th c.BC+]; especially feebleness, sickliness, [Refs 5th c.BC+]
__2 disease, sickness, [Refs 2nd c.BC+]; δι᾽ ἀσθένειαν[NT]
__3 ἀ. βίου poverty, [Refs 5th c.BC+]
__4 in moral sense, feebleness, weakness, τῆς ἀνθρωπίνης φύσεως[Refs 5th c.BC+] to be weak, feeble, sickly, ἀ. μέλη to be weak in limb, [Refs 5th c.BC+]; ἀ. ἀσθένειαν [NT+5th c.BC+]; ἠσθένησε he fell sick, [Refs 4th c.BC+]sick man, [Refs 5th c.BC+]; ἠσθενηκότα[Refs 2nd c.BC+]
__2 to be needy, [Refs 5th c.BC+]; ἠσθενηκότες, of those unable to pay taxes, [Refs 1st c.BC+]
__3 with infinitive, to be too weak to do a thing, not to be able.., [Refs 1st c.AD+]
__4 decline, ἠσθένησεν ἡ ἡμέρα εἰς τὴν ἑσπέραν[LXX]

terms.medium_def:
weak, ill (of physical weakness or illness, also of moral or spiritual weakness) 
without strength, weak, infirm, Mt. 26:41; Mk. 14:38; 1Pet. 3:7; helpless, Rom. 5:6; imperfect, inefficient, Gal. 4:9; feeble, without energy, 2Cor. 10:10; infirm in body, sick, sickly, ill Mt. 25:39, 43, 44; weak, mentally or spiritually, dubious, hesitating, 1Cor. 8:7, 10; 9:22; 1Thess. 5:14; afflicted, distressed, oppressed with calamities, 1Cor. 4:10
terms.lsj_entry:
ἀσθεν-ής, ές, 
without strength, weak, 
__1 in body, feeble, sickly, τοὺς ἀσθενέας τῆς στρατιῆς[Refs 5th c.BC+]; τοὺς ἀσθενεστάτους ἐς τὰς ταλαιπωρίας least able to bear hardship, [Refs 5th c.BC+]; ἀσθενέστερος πόνον ἐνεγκεῖν too weak to.., [Refs 4th c.BC+]. adverb ἀσθενῶς, ἴσχειν[Refs 5th c.BC+]
__2 in mind, and the like, τὸ ἀ. τῆς γνώμης the weakness, [Refs 5th c.BC+]
__3 in power, weak, feeble, ἀ. δύναμις[Refs 5th c.BC+]
__4 in property, weak, poor, οἱ χρήμασιν ἀσθενέστεροι[Refs 5th c.BC+]; οἱ ἀσθενέστεροι the weaker sort, i.e. the poor, [Refs 5th c.BC+]
__5 insignificant, οὐκ ἀσθενέστατος σοφιστὴς Ἑλλήνων[Refs 5th c.BC+]; paltry, ἀ. σόφισμα[Refs 4th c.BC+]; of streams, petty, small, [Refs 5th c.BC+]; of fluids, of small specific gravity, [Refs]; ἐς ἀσθενὲς ἔρχεται comes to nothing, [Refs]. adverb -νῶς feebly, without energy, [Refs 5th c.BC+]; on slight evidence, ἀπαγγέλλεσθαι[Refs 1st c.AD+]Praef.: comparative ἀσθενεστέρως, ἐπιθυμεῖν[Refs 5th c.BC+] -έστερον [Refs] -έστερα [Refs 5th c.BC+]

terms.medium_def:
to be weak, ill 
to be weak, infirm, deficient in strength; to be inefficient, Rom. 8:3; 2Cor. 13:3; to be sick, ill Mt. 25:36; metaphorically to be weak in faith, to doubt, hesitate, be unsettled, timid, Rom. 14:1; 1Cor. 8:9, 11, 12; 2Cor. 11:29; to be deficient in authority, dignity, or power, be contemptible, 2Cor. 11:21; 13:3, 9; to be afflicted, distressed, needy, Acts 20:35; 2Cor. 12:10; 13:4, 9

verses.esv_text: Joh 5:3 In these lay a multitude of invalids — blind , lame , and paralyzed .

verses.esv_text: Phili 2:27 Indeed he was ill , near to death . But God had mercy on him , and not only on him but on me also , lest I should have sorrow upon sorrow .

terms.medium_def:
to be weak, ill 
to be weak, infirm, deficient in strength; to be inefficient, Rom. 8:3; 2Cor. 13:3; to be sick, ill Mt. 25:36; metaphorically to be weak in faith, to doubt, hesitate, be unsettled, timid, Rom. 14:1; 1Cor. 8:9, 11, 12; 2Cor. 11:29; to be deficient in authority, dignity, or power, be contemptible, 2Cor. 11:21; 13:3, 9; to be afflicted, distressed, needy, Acts 20:35; 2Cor. 12:10; 13:4, 9
terms.lsj_entry:
Included with: ἀσθέν-εια, ἡ, 
want of strength, weakness, [Refs 5th c.BC+]; especially feebleness, sickliness, [Refs 5th c.BC+]
__2 disease, sickness, [Refs 2nd c.BC+]; δι᾽ ἀσθένειαν[NT]
__3 ἀ. βίου poverty, [Refs 5th c.BC+]
__4 in moral sense, feebleness, weakness, τῆς ἀνθρωπίνης φύσεως[Refs 5th c.BC+] to be weak, feeble, sickly, ἀ. μέλη to be weak in limb, [Refs 5th c.BC+]; ἀ. ἀσθένειαν [NT+5th c.BC+]; ἠσθένησε he fell sick, [Refs 4th c.BC+]sick man, [Refs 5th c.BC+]; ἠσθενηκότα[Refs 2nd c.BC+]
__2 to be needy, [Refs 5th c.BC+]; ἠσθενηκότες, of those unable to pay taxes, [Refs 1st c.BC+]
__3 with infinitive, to be too weak to do a thing, not to be able.., [Refs 1st c.AD+]
__4 decline, ἠσθένησεν ἡ ἡμέρα εἰς τὴν ἑσπέραν[LXX]

verses.esv_text: 1Cor 8:11 And so by your knowledge this weak person is destroyed , the brother for whom Christ died .
verses.preview_html: 1Cor 8:11 And so by your knowledge this weak person is destroyed, the brother for whom Christ died.

verses.esv_text: 1Cor 8:12 Thus , sinning against your brothers and wounding their conscience when it is weak , you sin against Christ .
verses.preview_html: 1Cor 8:12 Thus, sinning against your brothers and wounding their conscience when it is weak, you sin against Christ.

verses.esv_text: 2Cor 11:21 To my shame , I must say , we were too weak for that! But whatever anyone else dares to boast of —I am speaking as a fool — I also dare to boast of that .
verses.preview_html:
2Cor 11:21 To my shame, I must say, we were too weak for that!

But whatever anyone else dares to boast of—I am speaking as a fool—I also dare to boast of that.

verses.esv_text: 2Cor 11:29 Who is weak , and I am not weak ? Who is made to fall , and I am not indignant ?
verses.preview_html: 2Cor 11:29 Who is weak, and I am not weak? Who is made to fall, and I am not indignant?

verses.esv_text: 2Cor 12:10 For the sake of Christ , then, I am content with weaknesses , insults , hardships , persecutions , and calamities . For when I am weak , then I am strong .
verses.preview_html: 2Cor 12:10 For the sake of Christ, then, I am content with weaknesses, insults, hardships, persecutions, and calamities. For when I am weak, then I am strong.

verses.esv_text: 2Cor 13:3 since you seek proof that Christ is speaking in me . He is not weak in dealing with you, but is powerful among you .
verses.preview_html: 2Cor 13:3 since you seek proof that Christ is speaking in me. He is not weak in dealing with you, but is powerful among you.

verses.esv_text: 2Cor 13:4 For he was crucified in weakness , but lives by the power of God . For we also are weak in him , but in dealing with you we will live with him by the power of God .
verses.preview_html: 2Cor 13:4 For he was crucified in weakness, but lives by the power of God. For we also are weak in him, but in dealing with you we will live with him by the power of God.

verses.esv_text: 2Cor 13:9 For we are glad when we are weak and you are strong . Your restoration is what we pray for.
verses.preview_html: 2Cor 13:9 For we are glad when we are weak and you are strong. Your restoration is what we pray for.

verses.esv_text: Act 20:35 In all things I have shown you that by working hard in this way we must help the weak and remember the words of the Lord Jesus , how he himself said , ‘It is more blessed to give than to receive .’ ”

verses.esv_text: Rom 14:2 One person believes he may eat anything , while the weak person eats only vegetables .
